message:
Add comprehensive testing guide

Covers: unit tests, installation, live testing checklist (10 scenarios),
headless CI testing, error monitoring, and known issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,214 @@
+# Mutter Butter — Testing Guide
+
+## Unit Tests (no GNOME Shell required)
+
+Run with GJS standalone:
+
+```bash
+gjs -m tests/gridEngine.test.js
+gjs -m tests/stampPlacer.test.js
+gjs -m tests/focusTracker.test.js
+```
+
+Or all at once:
+
+```bash
+gjs -m tests/gridEngine.test.js && gjs -m tests/stampPlacer.test.js && gjs -m tests/focusTracker.test.js
+```
+
+These test the pure-logic modules: grid cell math, placement strategies, and focus rank tracking.
+
+## Installation
+
+### From source (development)
+
+```bash
+cd /home/jesse/git/mutter-butter
+zip -r /tmp/mutter-butter.zip metadata.json extension.js prefs.js stylesheet.css lib/ schemas/ -x "schemas/gschemas.compiled"
+gnome-extensions install /tmp/mutter-butter.zip --force
+glib-compile-schemas ~/.local/share/gnome-shell/extensions/mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io/schemas/
+```
+
+After installing, **log out and back in** to make GNOME Shell pick up the new extension (Wayland does not support hot-reloading new extensions).
+
+### Enable
+
+```bash
+gnome-extensions enable mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
+```
+
+### Disable
+
+```bash
+gnome-extensions disable mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
+```
+
+### Reinstall after code changes
+
+```bash
+cd /home/jesse/git/mutter-butter
+zip -r /tmp/mutter-butter.zip metadata.json extension.js prefs.js stylesheet.css lib/ schemas/ -x "schemas/gschemas.compiled"
+gnome-extensions install /tmp/mutter-butter.zip --force
+glib-compile-schemas ~/.local/share/gnome-shell/extensions/mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io/schemas/
+```
+
+Then disable and re-enable the extension (no logout required for updates to an already-known extension):
+
+```bash
+gnome-extensions disable mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
+gnome-extensions enable mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io
+```
+
+## Live Testing Checklist
+
+### 1. Grid Placement
+
+**Setup:** Enable the extension. Open a few windows.
+
+**Expected:**
+- [ ] Each window snaps to a grid-aligned position on the screen
+- [ ] Windows don't overlap
+- [ ] The grid respects the panel (top bar) — windows don't hide behind it
+- [ ] Opening a new window finds a free grid cell, or shrinks the lowest-ranked window to make room
+
+**Default grid:** 8 columns x 6 rows on a 1920x1080 monitor (240px x ~175px cells, accounting for panel).
+
+### 2. Auto-Shrink (Stamps)
+
+**Setup:** Default focus-rank threshold is 3. Open 4 or more windows.
+
+**Expected:**
+- [ ] The 4th most-recently-focused window shrinks to a 25%-scale live preview (a "stamp")
+- [ ] The stamp is a small, live-rendered clone of the original window content
+- [ ] The stamp appears in the grid (exact position depends on placement mode — default is "in-place")
+- [ ] The real window behind the stamp is invisible (opacity 0) but still shows in Alt+Tab
+- [ ] As you focus different windows, the least-recently-used ones auto-shrink
+
+### 3. Click-to-Restore
+
+**Setup:** Have at least one auto-shrunk stamp visible.
+
+**Expected:**
+- [ ] Clicking the stamp restores it to full size at its previous grid position
+- [ ] The previously lowest-ranked full-size window gets bumped to a stamp
+- [ ] Only one window is bumped per click (no cascade)
+
+### 4. Manual Zoom
+
+**Setup:** Focus any window.
+
+**Expected:**
+- [ ] `Super+KP_Add` zooms the window in (increases scale: 25% -> 50% -> 75% -> 100%)
+- [ ] `Super+KP_Subtract` zooms the window out (decreases scale: 100% -> 75% -> 50% -> 25%)
+- [ ] Manually-zoomed windows stay at their set scale even when focused
+- [ ] Zooming back to 100% makes the window auto-shrink-eligible again
+
+### 5. Placement Modes
+
+**Setup:** Open preferences (`gnome-extensions prefs mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io`).
+
+**In-Place (default):**
+- [ ] Stamps stay at their original grid position, just smaller
+
+**Shelf (set Stamp Placement to "Shelf"):**
+- [ ] Stamps migrate to the bottom edge (or whichever edge is configured)
+- [ ] Stamps line up along the shelf edge
+- [ ] When shelf is full, overflow stamps fall back to in-place
+
+**Pack (set Stamp Placement to "Pack"):**
+- [ ] Stamps fill available gaps in the grid
+
+### 6. Overview Integration
+
+**Setup:** Have some stamped windows, then press `Super` to open Activities Overview.
+
+**Expected:**
+- [ ] The Overview shows all windows normally (stamps temporarily restored to real actors)
+- [ ] Closing the Overview restores the stamp state
+- [ ] Clicking a window in the Overview works normally
+
+### 7. Window Lifecycle
+
+**Expected:**
+- [ ] Closing a window frees its grid cells
+- [ ] If closing reduces the count below the threshold, auto-shrunk windows restore
+- [ ] Minimizing a window removes it from the grid and destroys its stamp
+- [ ] Unminimizing re-places it in the grid
+- [ ] Fullscreen windows bypass the grid entirely
+- [ ] Maximized windows occupy the full grid and don't auto-shrink
+
+### 8. Drag and Drop
+
+**Full-size window drag:**
+- [ ] Dragging a full-size window shows a grid overlay with highlighted target cells
+- [ ] Releasing snaps the window to the target grid cell
+
+**Stamp drag:**
+- [ ] Pressing and dragging a stamp (hold + move past 8px threshold) starts a drag
+- [ ] The stamp follows the cursor during drag
+- [ ] Releasing snaps the stamp to the target grid cell
+- [ ] A simple click (no drag) on a stamp activates/restores it instead
+
+### 9. Preferences
+
+Open preferences: `gnome-extensions prefs mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io`
+
+- [ ] Grid page shows monitor selector (if extension is running)
+- [ ] Changing columns/rows values is possible (takes effect on extension restart for v1)
+- [ ] Behavior page has all controls: threshold, placement, shelf edge, shelf depth, hysteresis
+- [ ] Keybindings section shows zoom in/out keybind editors
+- [ ] Exempt Apps section allows adding/removing app IDs
+
+### 10. Disable/Enable Cycle
+
+- [ ] Disabling the extension removes all stamps and restores all windows to visible
+- [ ] Windows remain at their grid-assigned positions after disable
+- [ ] Re-enabling the extension re-grids all windows and starts tracking focus again
+- [ ] No errors in `journalctl -f /usr/bin/gnome-shell` during enable/disable cycle
+
+## Monitoring Errors
+
+Watch the GNOME Shell log in a terminal while testing:
+
+```bash
+journalctl -f /usr/bin/gnome-shell
+```
+
+Any JS errors from the extension will show with stack traces referencing files in `~/.local/share/gnome-shell/extensions/mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io/`.
+
+## Headless Integration Testing
+
+For automated CI or testing without a display:
+
+```bash
+dbus-run-session bash -c '
+    gnome-shell --headless --virtual-monitor 1920x1080 --no-x11 2>/dev/null &
+    sleep 6
+    gdbus call --session --dest org.gnome.Shell.Extensions \
+        --object-path /org/gnome/Shell/Extensions \
+        --method org.gnome.Shell.Extensions.EnableExtension \
+        "mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io"
+    sleep 2
+    gdbus call --session --dest org.gnome.Shell.Extensions \
+        --object-path /org/gnome/Shell/Extensions \
+        --method org.gnome.Shell.Extensions.GetExtensionInfo \
+        "mutter-butter@mutter-butter.github.io"
+    kill %1
+'
+```
+
+**Limitations of headless testing:**
+- The Overview starts active, which suspends stamp behavior
+- `first-frame` signal may not fire (the extension has a 500ms fallback timeout)
+- Clone rendering can't be visually verified
+- No user input possible (can't test drag, clicks, or keybinds)
+
+Headless testing verifies: extension loads without errors, grid initialization, window-created signal handling, enable/disable lifecycle.
+
+## Known Issues / Expected Behavior
+
+- Windows at 25% scale are very small — this is by design (postage stamps)
+- `Clutter.Clone` may show black rectangles for video players or GL-accelerated content (DMA-BUF subsurface limitation, same as GNOME's Overview)
+- Connector names are port-based, not monitor-based — moving a monitor to a different port creates a new grid config
+- Exemption is per-app, not per-window
+- Grid dimension changes in preferences take effect on extension disable/enable, not immediately
